entry.title: The Raven Roosts in the Cornflower Blue
entry.summary:
Author: Aurora2Sunset

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 75,940Chapters: 15/30Kudos: 415

Summary: Someone new moved to Smallville, in the house on the other side of the Kent farm. Clark meets the new kid, Bruce Wayne, and his first impression isn't great. He thinks that Bruce is spoiled and rude and out of place. Maybe that's why he blows up at him. But he still saves him when he's in trouble, that's just the kind of person that Clark is. After a while they develop a tentative kind of friendship over the summer. Bruce isn't like anyone else that Clark knows, and he feels connected to him in all of his out of place-ness. His own feelings for the other take him by surprise, he only hopes that he can stomp them down before he ruins their friendship over them. - Smallville AU where Bruce spends the summers there and befriends Clark long before either of them become superheroes.

entry.title: Keeping Tabs (Or Clark journals about his best friend)
entry.summary:
Author: Not_BootyShakker9000

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 26,912Chapters: 11/?Kudos: 165

Summary: Clark had used writing to understand himself ever after his powers first appeared, he just didn't expect his little coping mechanism to turn in a new way of looking at his quickly forming crush. ---- Aka. Clark is a weirdo who writes everything he notices about Batman until he actually notices he's in love.

entry.title: I'm hoping that you won't ever leave
entry.summary:
Author: naydene_is_ICONIC

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,267Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 9

Summary: Superman has been injured in a battle-- luckily, he has a certain Bat to watch over him while he heals. ORRRR a quick fluffy superbat fic following the prompt "you make the cutest face while concentrating and I just blurted it out / checking for injury with a gentle tough that lingers. I'm hoping that you won't ever leave / 'cause there's nowhere else I want to go Thank you "Kind Of" by Faye Webster for this title!! You can't stop me from using lyrics as titles...

entry.title: But you can't take the circus from the boy
entry.summary:
Author: Gotham_Blight

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 25,000Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 287

Summary: Damian Wayne has everything he's ever wanted. A beautiful husband. A precious child. A title of his own. Even his siblings, having experienced horrors beyond their years, are growing as people alongside him. But with everything in his life moving so smoothly, he can't help but wonder when the other shoe will drop. Happiness isn't something that comes easily to Demons, after all.

entry.title: Nowhere to Land
entry.summary:
Author: bloodvvitch

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 68,765Chapters: 20/25Kudos: 424

Summary: Bruce – recently injured and working a case that just won’t break – sees the tactical advantages of temporarily ‘dating’ Superman. Clark – who was almost too late to save his friend from a fatal fall – doesn’t understand what he’s signed up for. For two people who know each other so well, the World’s Finest just can’t manage to get on the same page.

entry.title: raison d'être
entry.summary:
Author: ilovepinkandcoffee

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 48,146Chapters: 13/?Kudos: 517

Summary: (n.) a reason for existing A year after the Riddler’s attack on Gotham, Batman struggles to repair a city fractured and drowning in crime. But when rumors spread through Gotham’s criminal underworld about an alien hero in Metropolis—an otherworldly figure with god-like powers who’s changing the course of history—Bruce can't help but be skeptical. But his concerns are confirmed when this so-called "Superman," arrives in Gotham one night during his patrol, bringing with him a wave of chaos that threatens to disrupt the fragile balance he has fought so hard to maintain. Clark had heard about Gotham City when he first arrived at Metropolis—a lawless, crime-ridden city plagued with corruption—but he had never felt the need to enter its borders. That is until he learns of a mysterious vigilante known as "The Batman," a figure who, like him, seeks justice for his city and its people, his curiosity gets the better of him. Drawn to the idea of meeting someone with a shared mission, Clark decides to seek out the elusive hero, after all, nothing bad can come out of it can it? But boy was he wrong.

entry.title: The Detective’s Greatest Blindspot
entry.summary:
Author: Tigerphoenix

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,138Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 3

Summary: While he just wants Superman to stay on his side of the bay, Bruce encounters reporter Clark Kent at a gala, who calls Bruce out on his "Brucie" act. What starts as a tense "what are you hiding?" case turns into a genuine connection, where Bruce is so convinced that Clark Kent and the alien savior of Metropolis could never be the same person, he misses Clark's biggest secret. Then disaster strikes. In a Superman fight, the Daily Planet is destroyed, and witnesses say Clark was inside. Clark is dead—Superman's negligence killed him—and it has turned the Bat into a hunter, looking for a way to make the god pay for killing the best man he had ever known.

entry.title: congratulations, you adopted a spider!
entry.summary:
Author: maraudersmqry

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 186,107Chapters: 29/31Kudos: 4,525

Summary: Peter was meant to be forgotten, he didn’t think that meant being dropped in Gotham. Or in his eight-year-old body, in the least. Or with vigilantes trying to adopt him! IN WHICH, Dr. Strange gave Peter one last chance at a life, instead of being emo around the faces who no longer recognized him.

entry.title: operation taming (romancing) the red hood
entry.summary:
Author: alleousai

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,615Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 125

Summary: The last thing Peter needed was a multiversal turn-around in the middle of America's Crime Capital, with his New York and secret identity at risk and his mental health down the drain. Lost and alone, he finds an abandoned coffee shop in Crime Alley and decides to make a home (and hideout) out of it until further notice, before a big guy dressed down in leather and quoting Jane Austen stumbles inside and demands recompense. One day is fine. Until the city's eyes are on him and the once-run down coffee shop becomes a haven for superheroes and civilians and metahumans alike, and all Peter wants to do is go back home and save New York. Tangled in a web of adventure, secrets and lies, the Red Hood won't let him go, nor will the Batfamily. Peter detonating won't be too bad of an idea, right? He couldn't be further from the truth. And Jason Todd trusted him enough to put him back together.

entry.title: This is a love story - no, this is a detective story
entry.summary:
Author: extraterrestrialcat

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,665Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 4

Summary: Clark and Bruce have a little something going on until Clark tears it apart, along with Bruce's heart. They attempt a new trial run for their relationship, and embrace after long periods of prolonged silence with passionate, fiery love. A bit of angst at the start, after all, you can't expect their relationship to be completely perfect. I absolutely hate angst so it will not have hefty amounts, I can assure you that.

entry.title: New Way Home
entry.summary:
Author: no_stranger

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 28,799Chapters: 9/?Kudos: 204

Summary: Peter loses Tony Stark? Check. Peter loses Aunt May? Check. Peter has to leave behind his universe, thus losing the last three people that really mattered to him? Check. Surely things can only go up from here.

entry.title: Love Me For Forever
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 13,484Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 8

Summary: Bruce Wayne died destroying a Xenomorph Queen. Clark Kent mourned him with Kryptonian ritual and carried the key to the Batcave against his sternum for nine days. Then Jason Todd brought Bruce back. Resurrection via Lazarus Pit is never clean. Bruce returns with his memories intact and a rage that displaces every other emotion. The two-meter distance that kept Clark alive in the hive becomes the boundary that keeps Bruce from violence. The team fractures over the ethics of resurrection without consent. And in the Pacific Northwest, something new is hunting—parasitic neural infiltrators that enter through the ear canal and leave their hosts conscious during control. Bruce volunteers as bait. Clark shadows him every meter. The key stays cold against Clark's chest. And somewhere in the Cascade Range, a Yautja marks Batman as worthy prey. "You were wrong to do it. But I'm glad you did." Love that persists despite impossibility. Love that consumes despite resistance. Love that exists in darkness and is overcome by forces larger than itself. This is what remains.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings
